title: High Availability: Multi-Container ReplicaSet
description:
Deploy 2 replicas of a sidecar logging pattern — a writer container and a shipper container sharing an emptyDir volume inside each Pod.

**Focus**: Multi-container Pod specs work inside ReplicaSet templates unchanged. Each replica gets its own independent emptyDir volume (not shared across replicas).

## Common Pitfalls
- **emptyDir is per-Pod**: Data is not shared between replicas, only between containers within one Pod.
- **Container name mismatch**: `kubectl logs <pod> -c <container>` requires the exact container name.
- **Sidecar not reading**: Verify volumeMount paths match between writer and shipper.
steps: Set selector.matchLabels and template.metadata.labels to the same label
Add a second container named 'shipper' using busybox image
Mount the 'log-data' volume in the shipper container at a different path
Configure shipper to continuously tail the log file written by writer
Apply and verify both containers in each pod reach Running state
hints: volumes go under spec.template.spec — same level as containers, not at RS top level
shipper needs a volumeMount pointing to the same volume name as writer, but mountPath can differ
Use 'tail -f' not 'tail' — without -f the process exits immediately and the pod crashloops
Use 'logger' as the app label value — validation queries pods with app=logger

editor_config.initial_code:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ReplicaSet
metadata:
  name: ha-logger
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: # TODO: use 'logger'
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: logger
    spec:
      volumes:
      - name: log-data
        emptyDir: {}
      containers:
      - name: writer
        image: busybox
        volumeMounts:
        - name: log-data
          mountPath: /var/log
        args: ['/bin/sh', '-c', 'while true; do date >> /var/log/app.log; sleep 5; done']
      # TODO: add shipper container (name: shipper, image: busybox)
      # Mount log-data volume at a different path and tail -f the log file

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ReplicaSet
metadata:
  name: ha-logger
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: logger
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: logger
    spec:
      volumes:
      - name: log-data
        emptyDir: {}
      containers:
      - name: writer
        image: busybox
        volumeMounts:
        - name: log-data
          mountPath: /var/log
        args: ['/bin/sh', '-c', 'while true; do date >> /var/log/app.log; sleep 5; done']
      - name: shipper
        image: busybox
        volumeMounts:
        - name: log-data
          mountPath: /out
        args: ['/bin/sh', '-c', 'tail -f /out/app.log']
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Apply the manifest
kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

# Wait for ReplicaSet to be ready
timeout=60
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  ready=$(kubectl get rs ha-logger -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  if [ "$ready" = "2" ]; then
    echo "✓ ReplicaSet has 2 ready replicas"
    
    # Get one of the pod names
    pod_name=$(kubectl get pods -l app=logger -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')
    
    # Wait a few seconds for logs to accumulate
    sleep 5
    
    # Check logs from shipper container
    logs=$(kubectl logs "$pod_name" -c shipper --tail=3 2>/dev/null)
    
    if echo "$logs" | grep -q "."; then
      echo "✓ Shipper container successfully reading logs from shared volume"
      
      # Verify both containers exist in each pod
      containers=$(kubectl get pod "$pod_name" -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[*].name}')
      if echo "$containers" | grep -q "writer" && echo "$containers" | grep -q "shipper"; then
        echo "✓ Both writer and shipper containers present in pods"
        exit 0
      fi
    fi
  fi
  sleep 2
  ((elapsed+=2))
done

echo "✗ ReplicaSet validation failed"
kubectl get rs ha-logger
kubectl get pods -l app=logger
exit 1
